evidence_quotes.quote: these publishers have the ability to effectively delete the games they have once sold to customers

paragraphs.text: Good day, I appreciate your efforts to create a safer and fairer digital world. I would like to suggest another issue that this initiative could tackle, an issue that I believe will fit well among your current goals. Video games are a massive industry that is not very well regulated. These days, giant publishers strive and reach for total control of the games they sell to the people. Because of the loosened legal grip, these publishers have the ability to effectively delete the games they have once sold to customers - this can be done by requiring the product to be connected to the company servers at all times, even during single player gameplay, then disabling access to the game. There may be many more ways to achieve this, that I have limited knowledge about. This is a tiny example of many invasive and unfair tactics that video game publishers use against the customers. Many video games have already been destroyed with little to no chance for preservation or recourse from the disadvantaged customers. I would like you to take a closer look at these practices. Many people across Europe feel the consequences of these exploits and fear the near future of the video game industry. Reaching out to the Digital Fairness Act is one of the few options we have available to encourage a positive change in this matter. Kindly, Bartosz.
